config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa dan tekanan mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan yang selalu menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has often been used to justify abuse by majorities, governments, or institutions. Individual rights are the whole point of constitutional protections here: free speech, due process, religious liberty, and limits on state power. That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, and public safety or public health can justify some limits, but those limits need to be narrow and carefully justified. If we put society first too easily, we risk turning "the common good" into an excuse to silence unpopular people or strip away basic freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan mayoritas, pembungkaman pendapat, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, aturan hukum, dan perlindungan yang jelas agar tidak berubah menjadi alat kekuasaan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang adil justru kuat karena mampu menjaga keseimbangan, bukan karena mengorbankan individu demi kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, that principle is exactly what has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach in the name of the "common good." Public safety and public health matter, but they only justify limited and clearly necessary restrictions, not open-ended power over people. A society is healthier when it protects individual rights first and then pursues the common good within those limits, not when it treats the person as disposable for the group.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau membenarkan keputusan yang tidak adil. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru harus dijalankan dengan batas yang jelas: hak dasar, hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan terhadap orang yang paling rentan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. In the United States, I have seen how appeals to the "common good" can become a cover for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the people affected are unpopular or politically weak. Public safety and public health can justify some limits, but those limits should be narrow, temporary, and tightly accountable. A free society is not one that treats individuals as expendable for the group; it is one that protects rights first and then pursues the common good within those boundaries.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
